title: Phegopteris barteriana
intro: Phegopteris barteriana, also known as the Bartered Maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents found in Phegopteris barteriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to treat various health conditions, and is also sometimes used in modern supplements and formulations for its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Phegopteris barteriana is classified within the plant family Thelypteridaceae. This family is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. Phegopteris barteriana, specifically, is an epiphytic fern native to Australia. As a member of the Thelypteridaceae family, it shares characteristics with other ferns in this group, such as a tropical to subtropical distribution. The classification of Phegopteris barteriana is further specified at the genus and species levels.
